categories.critical_security: Must fix before any deployment or public testing
categories.missing_features_critical: Core Bitcoin node functionality that is completely absent

items.title: Remove hardcoded testnet private key
items.description: A full testnet extended private key (tprv8ZgxMBicQKsPd7Uf69XL1XwhmjXhN7PBhGCAGrmcVgRN5nMSDxpmkZawt2CBrDDmFQchqid4LR) is hardcoded in main.rs lines 199-206, split across a format!() call. Any user who enables the wallet without providing descriptors shares this key. Since the repo is public on GitHub, anyone can sweep funds.
done_criteria: Hardcoded tprv key is completely removed from source code
Wallet refuses to start if no descriptors are provided in config
Clear error message tells user to provide external_descriptor and internal_descriptor
No private key material exists anywhere in the repository (grep -r 'tprv' returns nothing)
Optional: wallet can generate a fresh random descriptor on first run and persist it
testing_and_validation: Run 'grep -rn tprv crates/' and confirm zero matches
Run 'grep -rn xprv crates/' and confirm zero matches in non-test code
Start node with wallet.enabled=true and empty descriptors — must fail with descriptive error
Start node with wallet.enabled=true and valid descriptors — must succeed
Start node with wallet.enabled=false — must succeed without any descriptor requirement
Run 'git log -p | grep tprv' to confirm key is not in any future commits

items.title: Implement RPC authentication
items.description: The RPC server config defines user/password fields but they are never checked. Every endpoint (including stop, getpeerinfo, getmempoolinfo) is accessible to any TCP client with no authentication. Default config has rpc.enabled=true.
done_criteria: Axum middleware layer checks HTTP Basic auth on every RPC request
If config has user/password set, all requests without valid credentials get 401 Unauthorized
If config has no user/password, RPC server either refuses to start OR generates a random cookie file (like Bitcoin Core's .cookie)
Cookie file is written with restrictive permissions (0600)
Auth is checked before any handler logic executes
testing_and_validation: Send RPC request without auth header — must get 401
Send RPC request with wrong credentials — must get 401
Send RPC request with correct credentials — must get 200 with valid response
Test cookie-based auth flow if implemented
Verify no RPC method is reachable without authentication
Unit test: auth middleware rejects missing/invalid/valid credentials
Integration test: full RPC call with auth header

items.title: Fix CORS to use configured origins
items.description: When CORS origins are configured, the code applies CorsLayer::permissive() instead of using the actual configured origin list. This allows ANY website to make cross-origin RPC calls to a local node, exfiltrating peer lists and node state via JavaScript.
done_criteria: CorsLayer uses the actual origins from config.cors_origins
Only listed origins can make cross-origin requests
If cors_origins is empty, no CORS headers are added (deny all cross-origin)
Preflight OPTIONS requests are handled correctly for allowed origins
testing_and_validation: Send request with Origin header matching configured origin — CORS headers present
Send request with Origin header NOT in configured list — no CORS headers / request blocked
Send request with no cors_origins configured — no CORS headers added
Unit test: CORS layer construction from config origins
Test OPTIONS preflight returns correct Access-Control-Allow-Origin

items.title: Add inbound P2P handshake timeout
items.description: Outbound connections have a 10-second timeout but inbound accept() has NO timeout on the handshake. An attacker can open 125 TCP connections sending no data, exhausting all inbound slots permanently (slowloris attack).
done_criteria: Entire inbound handshake wrapped in tokio::time::timeout (10 seconds max)
Each individual message read within handshake also has a timeout
Connection is dropped cleanly if timeout expires
Timeout value is configurable or uses a sensible constant
testing_and_validation: Unit test: simulate slow client that sends no data — connection dropped after timeout
Unit test: simulate client that sends partial header (1 byte/sec) — connection dropped
Unit test: normal handshake within timeout — succeeds
Verify no resource leak on timeout (connection and socket properly closed)
Load test: open 200 connections sending no data — all timeout and free slots

items.title: Fix validated_height increment for duplicate blocks
items.description: When consensus engine reports a block as Duplicate, validated_height is incremented unconditionally. A malicious peer can re-send an already-validated block causing validated_height to jump past blocks that were never actually validated.
done_criteria: validated_height only increments on ProcessBlockResult::NewBlock
Duplicate blocks do not advance validated_height
OR: Duplicate blocks only advance height if the block hash matches expected block at validated_height+1
Rejected blocks trigger peer misbehavior scoring
testing_and_validation: Unit test: send same block twice — validated_height only increments once
Unit test: send block at wrong height as duplicate — validated_height does not change
Unit test: normal sequential blocks — validated_height increments correctly
Unit test: rejected block — validated_height unchanged, error logged

items.title: Validate network magic bytes on P2P message read
items.description: The read_message method does not validate that the 4-byte magic in bytes 0-3 matches the codec's configured network magic. A peer could send messages with wrong network magic that still decode successfully, enabling protocol confusion attacks.
done_criteria: read_message extracts magic bytes from header and compares against codec's network magic
Mismatched magic causes immediate disconnect with error
Both inbound and outbound message reads perform this check
testing_and_validation: Unit test: message with correct magic — decodes successfully
Unit test: message with wrong magic — returns error
Unit test: message with testnet magic on mainnet codec — returns error
Existing message round-trip tests still pass

items.title: Remove committed data/nodestore.redb from repository
items.description: data/nodestore.redb was committed before the .gitignore rule was added. May contain sync state, peer information, or operational data that should not be public.
done_criteria: File removed from git tracking with 'git rm --cached data/nodestore.redb'
.gitignore rule confirmed to cover data/ directory
File does not appear in future git status
testing_and_validation: Run 'git ls-files data/' and confirm no files listed
Run 'git status' and confirm data/nodestore.redb is not tracked
Verify .gitignore contains 'data/' entry

items.title: Implement transaction relay (inv/getdata/tx)
items.description: Transaction relay is completely missing. The node cannot receive, validate, or forward unconfirmed transactions. No handling for inv messages with MSG_TX type, no getdata requests for transactions, no tx message processing. This is fundamental Bitcoin node functionality.
done_criteria: Node handles incoming inv messages with MSG_TX inventory type
Node sends getdata to request announced transactions it doesn't have
Node processes incoming tx messages and adds valid transactions to mempool
Node announces new mempool transactions to peers via inv
Node responds to getdata requests for transactions in its mempool
Invalid transactions are rejected with appropriate reason
Already-known transactions are not re-requested
testing_and_validation: Unit test: receive inv with tx hash, verify getdata is sent
Unit test: receive tx message, verify it's added to mempool (if valid policy)
Unit test: receive duplicate tx inv, verify no second getdata
Unit test: receive invalid tx, verify rejection and no relay
Integration test: two-node tx relay (peer A announces → node requests → node receives → node announces to peer B)
Verify mempool.len() increases after receiving valid tx
Verify node sends inv to other peers after accepting tx

items.title: Implement key RPC methods (getblock, sendrawtransaction, getrawtransaction)
items.description: Critical RPC methods for interacting with the node are not implemented. getblock is needed to retrieve block data, sendrawtransaction to submit transactions, and getrawtransaction to look up transaction details.
done_criteria: getblock returns full block data (hex or JSON depending on verbosity param)
getblock with verbosity=0 returns raw hex
getblock with verbosity=1 returns decoded JSON with tx hashes
getblock with verbosity=2 returns decoded JSON with full tx details
sendrawtransaction accepts hex-encoded raw transaction, validates, adds to mempool, and relays
sendrawtransaction returns txid on success, error on failure
getrawtransaction returns raw tx hex or decoded JSON
All methods return proper JSON-RPC error codes on failure
testing_and_validation: Unit test: getblock with valid block hash returns correct data
Unit test: getblock with invalid hash returns JSON-RPC error
Unit test: getblock with different verbosity levels
Unit test: sendrawtransaction with valid tx returns txid
Unit test: sendrawtransaction with invalid tx returns error
Unit test: getrawtransaction with known txid returns data
Unit test: getrawtransaction with unknown txid returns error
Bitcoin Core compatibility: compare response format with Core's documentation

items.title: Implement peer keepalive (ping/pong)
items.description: No ping/pong messages are ever sent. The ping_nonce and last_ping fields exist in Peer struct but are never populated. Stale peers are never detected or evicted. Without keepalive, dead connections accumulate and waste slots.
done_criteria: Node sends ping messages to each peer every 120 seconds (configurable)
Node records the nonce and timestamp of sent pings
Node processes incoming pong messages and matches nonce to outstanding ping
Ping round-trip time is recorded per peer
Peers that don't respond to ping within 20 minutes are disconnected
Node responds to incoming ping messages with matching pong
Peer.last_ping and Peer.ping_nonce fields are properly used
testing_and_validation: Unit test: ping sent after interval elapsed
Unit test: pong received with correct nonce updates RTT
Unit test: pong received with wrong nonce is ignored
Unit test: peer that doesn't pong within timeout is disconnected
Unit test: incoming ping gets matching pong response
Verify peers_connected metric decreases when stale peer is evicted
Integration test: connect two nodes, verify ping/pong exchange

items.title: Implement peer ban enforcement
items.description: PeerStore has a full ban management system and Peer has record_misbehavior() that returns true at score >= 100. But: listen_inbound never checks if IP is banned, record_misbehavior is never called anywhere, bans are logged but never enforced.
done_criteria: listen_inbound checks PeerStore ban list before accepting connections
Banned IPs are rejected immediately at TCP accept
record_misbehavior() is called for: invalid headers, invalid blocks, protocol violations, message spam
When misbehavior score >= 100, peer is disconnected and IP is banned
Ban duration uses config.p2p.ban_duration_secs
Expired bans are cleared on lookup
PeerStore ban list is persisted across restarts
testing_and_validation: Unit test: banned IP rejected on inbound connection attempt
Unit test: misbehavior score accumulates across events
Unit test: score >= 100 triggers ban and disconnect
Unit test: expired ban allows reconnection
Unit test: ban persists across PeerStore reopen
Integration test: send invalid headers → peer gets banned → reconnect rejected

items.title: Implement peer reconnection logic
items.description: If peers disconnect, there is no automatic reconnection. The node relies entirely on initial DNS seed resolution and the configured connect list. Over time, the node may lose all peers with no way to recover without restart.
done_criteria: Background task periodically checks if outbound peer count < max_outbound
If below target, attempts to connect to known peers from PeerStore
Falls back to DNS seed re-resolution if PeerStore is exhausted
Implements exponential backoff for failed connection attempts
Respects ban list (does not reconnect to banned peers)
connect list peers are always retried on disconnect
testing_and_validation: Unit test: peer count drops below max_outbound → reconnection attempt triggered
Unit test: banned peer not retried
Unit test: exponential backoff increases delay after failures
Unit test: connect-list peer is retried immediately on disconnect
Integration test: disconnect all peers → node discovers new peers via DNS within 30s
Verify peer_count metric recovers after disconnect event

items.title: Add read timeout on established peer connections
items.description: run_peer_loop reads messages via conn.recv() with no timeout. If a peer completes handshake then stops sending data, the connection slot is occupied indefinitely. Combined with no ping/pong, stale connections accumulate.
done_criteria: conn.recv() wrapped in tokio::time::timeout
Timeout duration matches ping interval (e.g., 20 minutes)
Timeout triggers disconnect and slot cleanup
Timeout resets on every successfully received message
testing_and_validation: Unit test: peer sends no messages for 20 min → disconnected
Unit test: peer sends messages regularly → stays connected
Unit test: timeout resets after each message
Verify connection slot is freed after timeout disconnect

items.title: Fix sync peer selection (unverified start_height, i32→u64 cast)
items.description: Sync peer is selected based solely on self-reported start_height from version message. A negative i32 start_height wraps to a huge u64 value, always winning selection. Any attacker advertising high height becomes the exclusive sync peer.
done_criteria: start_height validated as non-negative (reject or clamp negative values)
start_height validated against reasonable maximum (e.g., current_height + 52560 for ~1 year ahead)
Use safe integer conversion (i32::try_from or u32::try_from) instead of 'as' cast
Sync peer selection considers multiple candidates, not just first one that's taller
If sync peer sends invalid data, it is replaced by another peer
testing_and_validation: Unit test: peer with negative start_height is not selected as sync peer
Unit test: peer with unreasonably high start_height is not selected
Unit test: peer with valid start_height > tip is selected
Unit test: sync peer replaced after sending invalid headers
Unit test: no integer overflow or truncation in height comparisons

items.title: Implement multi-peer sync with cross-validation
items.description: Entire sync depends on a single peer (sync_peer: Option<PeerId>). All headers and blocks come from one source. An attacker who becomes the sync peer controls the node's entire chain view. Bitcoin Core downloads from multiple peers and cross-validates.
done_criteria: Headers requested from at least 2-3 peers simultaneously
Headers from different peers are cross-validated (must agree on same chain)
Disagreeing peers are flagged and eventually banned
Block downloads distributed across multiple peers
Sync continues from other peers if one disconnects
Minimum cumulative proof-of-work threshold for accepting a headers chain
testing_and_validation: Unit test: headers from 2 peers agree → accepted
Unit test: headers from 2 peers disagree → both are re-requested from third peer
Unit test: single malicious peer cannot override honest majority
Unit test: sync peer disconnect → seamless failover to another peer
Unit test: blocks downloaded from multiple peers in parallel
Performance test: multi-peer sync faster than single-peer

items.title: Increase block download window
items.description: Block download batch size is 16 (BLOCK_DOWNLOAD_BATCH constant) vs Bitcoin Core's 1024. This severely limits sync speed, especially on high-latency connections. Each batch requires a full round-trip before the next can begin.
done_criteria: Block download window increased to at least 128 (configurable)
Pipeline allows requesting next batch before current batch is fully received
Track in-flight block requests to avoid duplicate requests
Timeout and re-request blocks that are not delivered within reasonable time
Adjust batch size based on peer bandwidth and latency
testing_and_validation: Benchmark: sync speed with batch=16 vs batch=128
Unit test: pipeline sends next batch when partial completion reached
Unit test: timed-out blocks are re-requested from different peer
Unit test: no duplicate block requests for in-flight blocks
Verify memory usage stays bounded with larger window

items.title: Enable BIP324 encrypted transport
items.description: wolfe-p2p has a bip324 module/crate but v2_transport is hardcoded to false. The README and info command claim BIP324 support. This is both a missing feature and misleading documentation.
done_criteria: BIP324 v2 handshake attempted when config.p2p.prefer_v2_transport is true
Graceful fallback to v1 if peer doesn't support v2
v2_transport flag accurately reflects actual transport negotiation result
Encrypted transport used for all messages after successful v2 handshake
v2 transport reported correctly in PeerInfo and RPC getpeerinfo
testing_and_validation: Unit test: v2 handshake with v2-capable peer succeeds
Unit test: v2 handshake with v1-only peer falls back gracefully
Unit test: messages encrypted/decrypted correctly over v2 transport
Unit test: v2_transport flag matches actual negotiation outcome
Integration test: connect to Bitcoin Core 28+ with v2 enabled

items.title: Wire PeerStore into runtime
items.description: PeerStore exists with full CRUD operations for known peers, ban management, and address storage, but is never used at runtime. Peer addresses from addr messages are not stored. Known peers are not used for reconnection.
done_criteria: Peer addresses from addr/addrv2 messages are stored in PeerStore
PeerStore queried for reconnection candidates when peer count is low
Ban list in PeerStore is checked on inbound connection accept
PeerStore persists across node restarts
getpeerinfo RPC includes data from PeerStore
testing_and_validation: Unit test: receive addr message → addresses stored in PeerStore
Unit test: reconnection logic queries PeerStore for candidates
Unit test: PeerStore data survives node restart
Integration test: learn peer via addr → restart node → reconnect to learned peer

items.title: Implement mempool UTXO input validation
items.description: Mempool add() only performs policy checks (size, fee rate, datacarrier). It does not validate that transaction inputs exist in UTXO set, signatures are valid, or transactions are consensus-valid. Required before enabling TX relay.
done_criteria: Transaction inputs validated against UTXO set before mempool acceptance
Script/signature verification performed (via libbitcoinkernel or equivalent)
Double-spend attempts detected and rejected
Fee computed from actual inputs minus outputs (not caller-provided)
Coinbase transactions rejected from mempool
Transactions spending non-existent UTXOs rejected
testing_and_validation: Unit test: tx with valid inputs and signatures accepted
Unit test: tx spending non-existent UTXO rejected
Unit test: tx with invalid signature rejected
Unit test: double-spend tx rejected
Unit test: coinbase tx rejected from mempool
Unit test: fee correctly computed from UTXO lookup

items.title: Add P2P message rate limiting
items.description: run_peer_loop forwards every received message with no rate limiting. A malicious peer can flood the node with valid but useless messages (thousands of inv, addr, or getheaders). No per-message-type throttling exists.
done_criteria: Per-peer message rate limit enforced (e.g., 1000 messages/minute)
Per-message-type rate limits (e.g., max 3 getheaders/minute, max 50 inv/second)
Peers exceeding rate limits get misbehavior score increase
Extreme flooding triggers immediate disconnect
Rate limits are configurable
testing_and_validation: Unit test: peer within rate limit — all messages processed
Unit test: peer exceeding global rate limit — disconnect triggered
Unit test: peer sending excessive inv — misbehavior score increases
Unit test: rate limits reset after time window
Load test: flood node with messages from one peer — node remains responsive to other peers

items.title: Implement wallet RPC endpoints
items.description: No wallet-related RPC methods exist. The wallet is initialized but users have no way to interact with it via RPC. Need getbalance, getnewaddress, listunspent, sendtoaddress, listtransactions at minimum.
done_criteria: getbalance — returns confirmed and unconfirmed balance
getnewaddress — returns a fresh receiving address
listunspent — returns list of unspent outputs
sendtoaddress — creates, signs, and broadcasts a transaction (requires TX relay FEAT-001)
listtransactions — returns recent wallet transactions
All methods return proper JSON-RPC errors when wallet is disabled
Bitcoin Core-compatible response formats
testing_and_validation: Unit test: getbalance returns correct balance
Unit test: getnewaddress returns valid Bitcoin address for configured network
Unit test: listunspent returns correct UTXOs
Unit test: wallet-disabled node returns appropriate error for all wallet RPCs
Integration test: getnewaddress → fund → getbalance shows funds
Compare response format with Bitcoin Core documentation

items.title: Implement PSBT creation in wallet
items.description: NodeWallet has sign() for PSBTs but no way to create them. Users cannot construct transactions without PSBT creation support. This is essential for any wallet functionality.
done_criteria: create_psbt() method that builds a PSBT from recipient address and amount
Coin selection algorithm (BDK provides this)
Change output handling with internal descriptor
Fee estimation (manual fee rate or from mempool)
createrawtransaction-compatible RPC endpoint
walletcreatefundedpsbt-compatible RPC endpoint
testing_and_validation: Unit test: create PSBT with valid recipient and amount
Unit test: PSBT includes change output when needed
Unit test: PSBT fee matches requested fee rate
Unit test: insufficient funds returns error
Integration test: create PSBT → sign → broadcast (with TX relay)

items.title: Wire apply_unconfirmed_txs to wallet
items.description: NodeWallet has apply_unconfirmed_txs() method but it is never called. The wallet only sees confirmed blocks, missing unconfirmed transactions entirely. Wallet balance won't show pending transactions.
done_criteria: When a new transaction enters the mempool, it's fed to wallet via apply_unconfirmed_txs()
Wallet balance reflects pending/unconfirmed transactions
When transactions are confirmed in a block, unconfirmed state is superseded
When transactions are evicted from mempool, wallet state is updated
testing_and_validation: Unit test: unconfirmed tx to wallet address shows as trusted_pending
Unit test: confirmed tx supersedes unconfirmed state
Unit test: evicted tx removed from wallet pending balance
Integration test: send tx → wallet shows pending → block confirms → wallet shows confirmed

items.title: Implement reorg handling in sync engine
items.description: NodeStore has full reorg support (remove_headers_above, etc.) but the sync engine has no reorg detection or handling. If the chain reorganizes, the node will get stuck or follow the wrong chain.
done_criteria: Detect when a peer sends headers that fork from our chain
Compare cumulative proof-of-work between competing chains
If new chain has more work: roll back to fork point, apply new chain
NodeStore headers rolled back via remove_headers_above()
Consensus engine chainstate updated to match reorg
Wallet notified of reorged blocks (unconfirm transactions)
Mempool re-accepts transactions from disconnected blocks
testing_and_validation: Unit test: detect fork at height N with competing chain
Unit test: reorg to chain with more cumulative PoW
Unit test: reject reorg to chain with less PoW
Unit test: rolled-back transactions return to mempool
Unit test: wallet balance updates correctly after reorg
Integration test: simulate 1-block reorg on regtest

items.title: Implement block pruning
items.description: StorageConfig has prune_target_mb field (0 = no pruning) but no pruning logic exists. For nodes with limited disk space, pruning old blocks while keeping recent ones and UTXO set is essential.
done_criteria: When prune_target_mb > 0, old block data is removed after validation
Keep at least 288 most recent blocks (Bitcoin Core minimum)
UTXO set and headers are never pruned
Pruned blocks cannot be served to peers (node signals NODE_NETWORK_LIMITED)
Disk usage stays within configured target
getblockchaininfo reports pruned=true and pruneheight
testing_and_validation: Unit test: blocks beyond threshold are pruned
Unit test: recent 288 blocks always retained
Unit test: headers not affected by pruning
Unit test: disk usage stays within target
Unit test: pruned node signals correct service bits

items.title: Implement log file output and format config
items.description: LoggingConfig has file and format fields but they are unused. format can be 'text' or 'json' but only text is ever used. file output is never configured.
done_criteria: When config.logging.format == 'json', logs are output in structured JSON format
When config.logging.file is set, logs are written to the specified file
File logging works alongside stdout logging
Log rotation or max file size should be considered
tracing-subscriber configured based on config values
testing_and_validation: Manual test: set format=json, verify JSON log output
Manual test: set file path, verify logs appear in file
Manual test: verify stdout still works when file logging is enabled
Unit test: logging config parsing for all format options

items.title: Fix peer metrics counters (underflow and inbound/outbound tracking)
items.description: peers_connected can underflow (u64 wraps to MAX on disconnect without connect). peers_inbound and peers_outbound are incremented on connect but never decremented on disconnect.
done_criteria: Use saturating_sub instead of fetch_sub for all peer counters
peers_inbound decremented on disconnect for inbound peers
peers_outbound decremented on disconnect for outbound peers
Track whether disconnecting peer was inbound/outbound (may need to store this in peer_addrs map)
Consider using peer_manager.peer_count() directly instead of separate counter
testing_and_validation: Unit test: disconnect without prior connect — counter stays at 0 (no underflow)
Unit test: inbound peer connect then disconnect — peers_inbound returns to 0
Unit test: outbound peer connect then disconnect — peers_outbound returns to 0
Verify Prometheus metrics show correct values after connect/disconnect cycle

items.title: Fix integer type consistency for block heights
items.description: The codebase mixes u64, u32, and i32 for block heights. Key dangerous casts: start_height (i32) as u64 wraps negatives; u64 as u32 silently truncates. Heights should use a consistent type with safe conversions.
done_criteria: Standardize on u32 for block heights throughout the codebase (Bitcoin max ~4 billion blocks)
All 'as' casts for heights replaced with checked conversions (u32::try_from, etc.)
Negative start_height from peers treated as 0 or rejected
No silent truncation possible in any height conversion path
testing_and_validation: Grep for 'as u32' and 'as u64' in height-related code — confirm all are safe or replaced
Unit test: negative i32 start_height handled safely
Unit test: very large u64 height conversion to u32 returns error (not truncation)
All existing tests pass with new type-safe conversions

items.title: Audit and fix unsafe Send/Sync on ConsensusEngine
items.description: ConsensusEngine has 'unsafe impl Send' and 'unsafe impl Sync' with a comment claiming libbitcoinkernel types are thread-safe. This needs verification. ConsensusEngine is shared via Arc across threads.
done_criteria: Thread safety of bitcoinkernel crate verified against upstream documentation
If thread-safe: document evidence in code comment with reference to upstream source
If NOT thread-safe: wrap ChainstateManager in Mutex or RwLock
Concurrent access test that exercises validate_block from multiple threads simultaneously
No data races under ThreadSanitizer (TSAN)
testing_and_validation: Review bitcoinkernel crate source for Send/Sync implementations
Stress test: call validate_block concurrently from 4+ threads
Run with RUSTFLAGS='-Z sanitizer=thread' if possible
Integration test: concurrent RPC queries while block validation is running

items.title: Fix metrics server (use proper HTTP, add connection limits)
items.description: Metrics endpoint is a raw TCP handler that writes HTTP response without reading the request. No path matching, no method validation, no connection timeout, no concurrent connection limit. Vulnerable to file descriptor exhaustion.
done_criteria: Use a proper HTTP server (axum or hyper) for metrics endpoint
Only respond to GET /metrics path
Return 404 for other paths
Set connection timeout
Limit concurrent connections
Properly close connections after response
testing_and_validation: Unit test: GET /metrics returns 200 with prometheus format
Unit test: GET /other returns 404
Unit test: POST /metrics returns 405
Load test: 1000 concurrent connections — server remains responsive
Verify metrics output format matches Prometheus text exposition format

items.title: Implement stop RPC that actually shuts down the node
items.description: The stop RPC method returns 'BitcoinWolfe server stopping' but doesn't actually stop anything. The method needs access to the shared shutdown AtomicBool to trigger graceful shutdown.
done_criteria: stop RPC method triggers graceful shutdown via shared AtomicBool
Node performs same cleanup as Ctrl+C (consensus interrupt, progress save)
RPC server sends response before shutting down
stop requires authentication (depends on SEC-002)
Shutdown is logged
testing_and_validation: Integration test: call stop RPC → node exits cleanly
Verify response is sent before shutdown
Verify consensus engine is interrupted
Verify sync progress is saved
Test that unauthenticated stop request is rejected (after SEC-002)

items.title: Implement self-connection detection via version nonce
items.description: Random nonce is generated in version message but incoming version nonces are never checked. The node can form connection loops to itself, wasting slots.
done_criteria: Store nonce used in outbound version messages
Check incoming version message nonce against stored outbound nonces
If nonce matches, disconnect immediately (self-connection detected)
Log self-connection detection at warn level
testing_and_validation: Unit test: version with matching nonce triggers disconnect
Unit test: version with different nonce proceeds normally
Unit test: multiple outbound nonces tracked correctly

items.title: Add wallet database encryption at rest
items.description: Wallet SQLite database opened with no encryption. Contains private key material (BDK stores descriptors including xprv keys). Any filesystem access can extract private keys.
done_criteria: Wallet database encrypted at rest (SQLCipher or BDK encryption support)
Encryption password derived from user-provided passphrase or generated key
File permissions set to 0600 on wallet database
Passphrase can be provided via config, environment variable, or interactive prompt
testing_and_validation: Unit test: wallet file is not readable as plain SQLite
Unit test: wallet opens with correct passphrase
Unit test: wallet fails to open with wrong passphrase
Verify file permissions are restrictive
Hex dump wallet file and confirm no plaintext key material visible

items.title: Fix network config to error on unknown chain strings
items.description: NetworkConfig::bitcoin_network() and chain_type_from_network() default unrecognized inputs to mainnet. A typo like 'maninet' silently starts the node on mainnet with real funds at risk.
done_criteria: Unrecognized network strings return an error (Result type) instead of defaulting to mainnet
Clear error message lists valid options: mainnet, testnet, testnet4, signet, regtest
Node refuses to start with invalid network string
Both bitcoin_network() and chain_type_from_network() updated
testing_and_validation: Unit test: valid strings (mainnet, testnet, signet, regtest) return correct network
Unit test: 'maninet' (typo) returns error
Unit test: empty string returns error
Unit test: case sensitivity handled (e.g., 'Mainnet' → either works or errors, not silent default)

items.title: Implement mempool ancestor/descendant limit enforcement
items.description: MempoolConfig has max_ancestors and max_descendants (both default 25) but these limits are never checked. The pool tracks tx count but not ancestor/descendant chains. This allows mempool flooding via long chains.
done_criteria: Track parent-child relationships between mempool transactions
Reject transactions that would exceed max_ancestors limit
Reject transactions that would cause any descendant to exceed max_descendants limit
Package/CPFP-aware limit checking
testing_and_validation: Unit test: chain of 25 ancestors accepted, 26th rejected
Unit test: tx with 25 descendants accepted, adding 26th descendant rejected
Unit test: diamond-shaped dependency graph counted correctly
Unit test: removing parent updates descendant counts

items.title: Implement mempool trim and expiry
items.description: MempoolConfig has max_size_mb and expiry_hours but trim() and expire logic are never called. The mempool will grow unbounded in memory.
done_criteria: Periodic trim check when mempool exceeds max_size_mb
Trim evicts lowest fee-rate transactions first
Transactions older than expiry_hours are removed
Trim and expire run periodically (e.g., every 60 seconds)
Evicted transactions not re-accepted until fee rate increases
testing_and_validation: Unit test: mempool at max size — new higher-fee tx evicts lowest-fee tx
Unit test: old transactions expired after expiry_hours
Unit test: trim maintains consistent state (no dangling references)
Unit test: eviction order is strictly by fee rate

items.title: Make user-agent string configurable
items.description: User-agent sent to every peer includes exact version. This enables targeted attacks against specific versions. Should be configurable for privacy-conscious operators.
done_criteria: P2pConfig gets optional user_agent field
If set, custom user-agent is used in version messages
If empty, default /BitcoinWolfe:VERSION/ is used
Maximum length enforced (256 bytes per BIP)
testing_and_validation: Unit test: default user-agent contains version
Unit test: custom user-agent used when configured
Unit test: overly long user-agent truncated
Verify connected peers see configured user-agent

items.title: Add cargo audit to CI pipeline
items.description: Dependencies use broad version ranges. No automated vulnerability scanning. A compromised minor release would be pulled on cargo update. CI should run cargo audit to catch known CVEs.
done_criteria: cargo audit added as CI step
CI fails on any known vulnerability in dependencies
Consider pinning exact versions for security-critical deps (bitcoin, rand, bitcoinkernel)
Dependabot or renovate configured for automated dependency updates
testing_and_validation: CI pipeline runs cargo audit successfully
Intentionally add a vulnerable dep version → CI fails
Verify critical deps (bitcoin, rand, bitcoinkernel) are pinned to exact versions in Cargo.toml

items.title: Fix CI clippy suppression
items.description: CI runs clippy with -A dead_code -A unused_imports which hides potentially important warnings. Dead code in security-critical software could mask unfinished features or unmaintained code paths.
done_criteria: Remove blanket -A dead_code and -A unused_imports from CI clippy
Fix all resulting dead_code warnings (remove unused code or add #[allow(dead_code)] with justification)
Fix all unused_imports warnings
CI clippy runs clean with no suppressions
testing_and_validation: cargo clippy --all-targets passes with no warnings and no -A flags
No new #[allow(dead_code)] added without a comment explaining why
CI pipeline succeeds
